contextual.title: Corticosteroids in Community-acquired Pneumonia
contextual.text:
Summary: The purpose of this study is to determine the efficacy of addition of corticosteroid therapy to antibiotics in children hospitalized with community-acquired pneumonia.~The hypothesis is that the use of corticosteroids decreases the length of stay in children hospitalized with community-acquired pneumonia.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 Children hospitalized for community-acquired pneumonia 

 2-18 years old 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 Pulmonary chronic disease (including asthma) 

 Immunodeficiency 

 Diseases wich requires corticosteroids therapy (i.e. rheumatic diseases) 

 Wheezing in current disease 

 Previous hospitalization (14 days prior to admission) 

 Pleural effusion on admission 

 Malnutrition

metadata.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to determine the efficacy of addition of corticosteroid therapy to antibiotics in children hospitalized with community-acquired pneumonia.~The hypothesis is that the use of corticosteroids decreases the length of stay in children hospitalized with community-acquired pneumonia.

Community-acquired Pneumonia.definition: Infection of the lungs in a patient who is not hospitalized or who has not resided in a long-term care facility for 14 days prior to the onset of symptoms. It is associated with some signs and symptoms of acute infection and is accompanied by the presence of an acute infiltrate on chest radiograph.(US Pharm. 2007;32(10):44-5 https://www.uspharmacist.com/article/community-acquired-pneumonia)
